{{Quote|That's the most magnificent thing I've ever seen, Urai.|[[Tyber Zann]] to [[Urai Fen]]|audio=Eclipse-FoC.flac|<ref name="FOC" />}}
The '''''Eclipse''''' was the first [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class dreadnought]] ever constructed and the lead [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and prototype of its class. It was built as a [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] for [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who used it after being [[Transfer essence|reborn]] into one of his [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] bodies. Both a powerful technological tool and a potent psychological weapon, the ''Eclipse'' boasted a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] worth of weaponry and was almost unstoppable.

The ''Eclipse'' was equipped with a bevy of weapons and other technologies, including [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]], but chief among its weapons was an [[Axial superlaser/Legends|axial superlaser]], capable of cracking a planet's crust or destroying a [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] in a single shot.

For much of its existence, the ''Eclipse'' was under construction around the planet [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]]. Crime lord [[Tyber Zann]] [[Battle for the Eclipse|raided]] the vessel from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] forces in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] to retrieve data from its memory banks, and left it shortly thereafter, realizing that it would present far too big of a target to both the Empire and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces. The ship was later moved to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], where the reborn Emperor Palpatine took control of it as his personal [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] and flagship. The ''Eclipse'' led his new [[Dark Empire]] in an [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|assault]] on the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] stronghold of [[Da Soocha V]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], and in the battle was destroyed by one of Palpatine's [[Force storm (wormhole)|Force storms]].

==Specifications==
{{Quote|I've never seen such an ambitious design… apart from the Death Star.|Tyber Zann|audio=AmbitiousDesign-FoC.flac|<ref name="FOC" />}}
The ''Eclipse'' was the lead [[Starship/Legends|ship]] of the 17.5 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class dreadnought]], the prototype of that class,<ref name="DESB" /> and one of only two ships of the class to ever be built.<ref name="FF">[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 125|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 125]]</ref> Encased in a [[Color/Legends|jet-black]] [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] and [[Quadranium/Legends|quadranium]]<ref name="egw" /> alloyed hull,<ref name="DESB" /> the immense ship was said to be the deadliest [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] in the galaxy.<ref name="trailer">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/videogames/news/eawfoctrailer.html|text=''Star Wars'' Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Trailer}}</ref> By volume, the ''Eclipse'' was one of the largest [[Warship/Legends|warships]] ever built<ref name="DESB" /> and the largest [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ever,<ref name="Byss">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planet1|series=Byss and the Deep Core|text=Part 1: Byss}}</ref> though in length it was somewhat shorter than the 19 kilometer [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnoughts]] and [[Vengeance-class dreadnought|''Vengeance''-class dreadnoughts]].<ref name="SOTG" /> The ship's hull was also designed to resemble naval warships of eras long past.<ref name="EGVV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

[[File:Eclipse_Bridge.jpg|thumb|left|The bridge tower of the ''Eclipse'']]
The interior of the ''Eclipse'' was similar to that of a Star Destroyer, though it was also outfitted with repair facilities and [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]] reminiscent of those found on [[Space station/Legends|space stations]]. The vessel had several war rooms suitable for high-level meetings of [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] staff.<ref name="Byss" /> Troop quarters were located near the bow, at the aft end of the keel-like structure. The fore launch bay and hangar sat approximately midships, and about 2.5 kilometers behind them lay the aft launch bay.<ref name="EGVV" /> The vessel had other, smaller hangars throughout the ship as well, suited for [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] and the like.<ref name="NEGVV" /><ref name="DE" /> The large hangars on the ''Eclipse'' were big enough to hold a [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]]. In practice, they were occupied by 600 [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]], organized into fifty [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], and 96 [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]], comprising eight squadrons.<ref name="DESB" /> The ''Eclipse''{{'s}} [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] escort alone could take on almost any [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] fleet.<ref name="NEGVV" />

In offensive weaponry, the ''Eclipse'' was outfitted with 550 heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], 500 [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], 75 [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and a [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] mounted along the spine of the ship,<ref name="DESB" /> the equivalent of a [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] worth of weaponry.<ref name="FOC" /> The ''Eclipse'' was one of the most heavily-armed starships ever built,<ref name="NEGVV" /> and was in fact capable of holding off an entire New Republic fleet by itself.<ref name="FF" /> Its fighting ability was unmatched, and were it not for the unconventional means by which it was destroyed it is likely that it would have been a formidable power for the Empire even without accompanying starships.<ref name="SOTG" /> Other combat-oriented technologies included ten [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]], able to prevent enemy ships from entering [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], 100 [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]], and an enhanced [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] system.<ref name="DESB" /> The shield systems, combined with the design of the hull were also powerful enough to be capable, without hesitation, of ramming enemy vessels and not suffer any major damage to the hull.<ref name="EGVV" />

Though the ''Eclipse'' was primarily designed for [[Space warfare/Legends|space combat]], the vessel was geared for ground assaults as well.<ref name="NEGVV" /> Every trooper stationed aboard the ''Eclipse'', 150,000 in total, was specially selected. The soldiers on the vessel included a [[CompForce/Legends|CompForce]] [[Assault battalion|Assault Battalion]], a newly created [[Legion/Legends|legion]] of [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guards]], and a group of [[Imperial Sovereign Protector]]s as [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] personal guard. Five [[IM-455 modular garrison|prefabricated garrison bases]] and 100 [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] rounded out the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} ground assault forces. 708,470 crew were assigned to the vessel as well, including 4,175 [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]], though the ship could run with as few as 88,500 crew members. The ''Eclipse'' had a tonnage of 600,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]], [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] capable of lasting 10 [[Standard year/Legends|years]],<ref name="DESB" /> and was entirely self-sufficient.<ref name="FF" />

The ship's communications tower sat atop the bridge, its main communications array jutted from the tower's side,<ref name="EGVV" /> and the long-range [[Comm unit/Legends|communications system]] extended downward from the prow.<ref name="NEGVV" /> The communications systems on the Eclipse were powerful enough to broadcast over all frequencies and drown out other signals, as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine did during his [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|invasion]] of the [[Cyax system/Legends|Cyax system]] in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="DE" /> Palpatine had a private command console on the vessel that could be used to access his personal records.<ref name="FOC" /> The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] tower of the ''Eclipse'' was asymmetric, with the main sensor array on the other side of the bridge from the communications array, and the deflector shield command perched on top of the bridge tower.<ref name="EGVV" />

An array of six [[Sublight engine/Legends|drive engines]] on the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} stern gave it a sublight acceleration of 940 G,<ref name="NEGVV" /> and the ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] was rated at 2x—the backup hyperdrive, 6x. Both sublight and hyperdrive were enhanced to give the ''Eclipse'' maximum reaction speed.<ref name="DESB" />

===Superlaser===
[[File:Esuper_laser2.jpg|thumb|The ''Eclipse'' destroys an [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] with its superlaser.]]
The most important development in the design of the ''Eclipse'' was its main weapon,<ref name="DESB" /> a concealed superlaser running along the spine of the ship.<ref name="NEGVV" /> Based on the technology of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]],<ref name="FF" /> the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} single superlaser could fire at two-thirds the power of one of the Death Star's superlaser components,<ref name="SOTG" /> a feat made possible by advances in superlaser focusing and generator technology. The weapon was capable of destroying even the most powerful [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]] and entire continents in an instant.<ref name="DESB" /> It could also crack the crust of planets<ref name="EGVV" /> and rip enemy capital ships apart with a single shot, although early in the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} construction, the laser was prone to breakdowns.<ref name="FOC" /> The superlaser was difficult to aim, as the entire vessel needed to be aligned with the target for it to do so.<ref name="SOTG" /> According to Admiral Motti in the field manual ''Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide'', the superlaser's primary purpose was to penetrate planetary shields as well as destroy cities in a single blast.<ref name="IHB">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>

==Role==
The ''Eclipse'' was equipped with the firepower of an entire planet,<ref name="FOC" /> making it a powerful foe in standard ship-to-ship combat. But the vessel's heavy [[Hull/Legends|hull]] plating and strong shields also made it possible to use the ship as a ramming device against virtually anything, destroying enemies through sheer impact force.<ref name="NEGVV" /> According to Admiral [[Conan Antonio Motti/Legends|Conan Antonio Motti]], the ''Eclipse'', alongside that of [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|its class]] as well as its sister ship class [[Sovereign-class Super Star Destroyer|''Sovereign'']], were intended in early development to be next-generation Star Dreadnoughts for the purpose of solidifying Imperial rule within the next century.<ref name="IHB" />

Though the ''Eclipse'' was a powerful warship in its own right, it was also designed as a psychological weapon. Its enormous size and black hull made for a powerful effect against enemies, and in battle it handily demoralized the opposing forces.<ref name="FF" /> The vessel's resemblance to ancient naval ships further enhanced its fearsome image.<ref name="EGVV" /> In addition to its use as a warship, the ''Eclipse'' also served for some time as an [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] space station above the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] capital of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. As well as being a constant symbol of Imperial dominance to the people of Byss, it was used as a meeting ground for officials in the Imperial Navy.<ref name="Byss" />

==History==
===Early construction===
{{Quote|You wanted to capture the Emperor's new toy? Fine. Let's see how long you can keep her.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] to Tyber Zann|audio=EmperorsNewToy-FoC.flac|<ref name="FOC" />}}
[[File:Eclipsepro.jpg|thumb|left|120px|The ''Eclipse'' under construction]]
Intended to serve as a symbol of Imperial power, the ''Eclipse'', alongside the ''Eclipse II'', were commissioned directly by Palpatine, designed to meet his exact specifications. The first of a new line of [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] and the most recent of the Empire's plethora of [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]],<ref name="DESB" /> the Empire began construction on the ''Eclipse'' around the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="SOTG" /> above Kuat. Although it was not the only prototype of the ''Eclipse''-class dreadnought, with another having been converted into the mobile superlaser platform known as the [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> it was the first of the class to be completed.<ref name="DESB" /> During the early phases of construction, Palpatine visited the vessel several times.<ref name="FOC" /> However, the ''Eclipse'' was later delayed in completion thanks to Palpatine's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|demise]] and the ensuing power vaccuum.

Crime lord [[Tyber Zann]], leader of the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]], first became aware of the ''Eclipse'' shortly after Yavin, while following up rumors of secret vaults of treasure belonging to Palpatine. Amidst the wreckage of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], Zann's crew retrieved [[data pod]]s with details and construction schedules of the ''Eclipse'', and believing that the records he sought were on the warship, Zann vowed to capture it.<ref name="FOC" /> By a few months after Yavin, Zann had already begun sending out [[Agent/Legends|agents]] to investigate the ''Eclipse'', an act that did not go unnoticed by the Empire.<ref name="Underworld">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref>

An armada of Imperial ships was assigned to guard the ''Eclipse'' at Kuat, and despite Zann's efforts to find a weakness in the Empire's [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]], it would be four years until he made his move. The crime lord discovered that the [[Keycard/Legends|pass key]] to Palpatine's command console on the ''Eclipse'' was kept in a vault in the [[Imperial Archives (museum)/Legends|Imperial Archives]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and even as Palpatine and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] went to the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] in preparation for the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Zann launched [[Raid on the Imperial Archive|his raid]] on the archives. He was successful, and it was not long before he seized his chance to take the ''Eclipse'' in the chaos that followed the Imperial loss at Endor. The ships that would normally be guarding the warship had to regroup upon their return from Endor, and so Zann took his forces to Kuat.<ref name="FOC" />

The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] sent a force to the system as well. Zann claimed he wanted to destroy the ''Eclipse'', and while the Alliance engaged the forces of the Empire, his fleet made their move towards the vessel. As a heated [[Battle for the Eclipse|battle]] broke out between the rebels and Empire, Zann's crew meanwhile took a [[Boarding craft/Legends|boarding shuttle]] onto the ''Eclipse'', wrested control of the warship, and brought the superlaser online. The Consortium turned the superlaser on the amassed fleets, and with their motives revealed, the rebels turned on them.<ref name="FOC" />

[[File:Tyber_Zann_Galaxy.jpg|thumb|right|Tyber Zann uses a command console on the ''Eclipse''.]]
As the conflict progressed, an Imperial fleet commanded by the ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought [[Annihilator (Executor-class)/Legends|''Annihilator'']] entered from hyperspace to aid the thinning Imperial forces, after the Rebel Alliance navy had retreated. Unfortunately for the Consortium, the superlaser failed at this moment. However, Zann's boarding party aboard the ''Eclipse'' was soon able to reactivate the superlaser and destroy much of the Imperial fleet, after which the Consortium fleet mopped up the rest. After retrieving the pertinent data from Palpatine's records he abandoned the ''Eclipse'' for the Empire to retake. Zann knew the ship was too big of a target for the Empire and Alliance to ignore, and so left it to the Imperial Navy to complete its construction.<ref name="FOC" />

===After Endor===
{{Quote|I've no need to keep the half-constructed Eclipse. And it presents a target even the Rebels couldn't miss.|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
The ''Eclipse'' was seen by many as emblematic of the problems with Palpatine's military policy. The Imperial naval planners did not approve of lavish spending on a single behemoth over several smaller ships of the line, seeing it as a self-aggrandizing waste, and after Palpatine's apparent [[Death/Legends|death]] in the Battle of Endor it was felt that this kind of purchasing could no longer be justified. Some even suggested scrapping it and using the freed resources to build more Star Destroyers. But Palpatine was not actually dead. While [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] believed him to have been killed at Endor, the Emperor had [[Transfer essence|escaped]] to a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body on Byss, and under his orders construction on the ''Eclipse'' continued.<ref name="DESB" />

[[File:Eclipse-NEGVV.png|thumb|left|The stern of the ''Eclipse'']]
Still, to many it seemed as if construction on the vessel would never end.<ref name="DESB" /> The ''Eclipse'' was said to have taken as long to construct as either of the Empire's Death Stars.<ref name="FF" /> Over the course of the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} lengthy construction the builders incorporated decades' worth of the latest achievements in technology, including the gravity well projectors, enhanced ion cannons, and improvements to the sublight engines and hyperdrive.<ref name="DESB" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8.5 ABY]], the half-completed ''Eclipse'' [[Undercover attack on Kuat|left]] Kuat for the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], used by the [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] staff to evacuate as [[Taking of Kuat|the planet fell]] to the New Republic.<ref name="EC" /> It became a permanent fixture in orbit around the world of Byss, where construction resumed, aided by [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] labor, and was eventually completed. In this time it served as an orbital [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], where [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admirals]] and other high-ranking members of the Imperial navy met to plan Imperial strategy.<ref name="Byss" /><ref name="TEA" /> From its deck, Palpatine commanded his fleets in their campaign against the New Republic.<ref name="EGVV" />

===Service===
{{Quote|Soon, my young apprentice, your sister will be mine!''"<br />"''Yes, my master.|Emperor Palpatine and Luke Skywalker aboard the ''Eclipse'' as it approaches Pinnacle Base|<ref name="DE Audio">[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
[[File:Eclipse_Allegiance.jpg|thumb|right|''Eclipse'', accompanied by a Star Destroyer and two ''Allegiance''-class battlecruisers, enters the Cyax system in 10 ABY.]]
In 10 ABY, Palpatine at last decided to make his move against the New Republic, and determined that the world of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] would be the first of the major Republic worlds to die. After an [[Battle of Calamari|initial assault]] on the planet with his [[World Devastator]]s, Palpatine planned to personally lead the final attack on Mon Calamari from the ''Eclipse''.<ref name="DESB" /> He would not get this chance though, as his commander, the defected Rebel [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], sabotaged the Devastators, giving victory of the battle to the New Republic.<ref name="DE" />

But the Emperor was determined to destroy those who opposed him. The ''Eclipse'' was deployed into other battles, often scattering enemy fleets through sheer fear without a single shot being fired. When Palpatine made it his personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the terrible reputation it already held was increased even further.<ref name="NEGVV" />

After the loss at Mon Calamari, Palpatine soon found a new target, the [[Pinnacle Base|Republic headquarters]] on [[Da Soocha V]]. Accompanied by several Star Destroyer escorts, including two [[Allegiance-class battlecruiser|''Allegiance''-class battlecruisers]],<ref name="audio">[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]]</ref><ref name="DE" /> the ''Eclipse'' leapt into the Cyax system, almost destroying a [[DP20 gunship/Legends|Corellian gunship]] as it plowed by the much smaller vessel.<ref name="DE" /> The New Republic instantly identified the massive vessel as Palpatine's flagship.<ref name="audio" />

[[File:Eclipse_Destruction.jpg|thumb|left|The destruction of the ''Eclipse'']]
As the Rebels began broadcasting emergency commands, the ''Eclipse''{{'s}} communications systems cut across all frequencies, transmitting a message from Emperor Palpatine to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] that demanded she board his shuttle and be taken to the ''Eclipse''. Leia was brought to a small hangar near the bow of the vessel, and was escorted off the shuttle to meet the Emperor himself. Through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Leia felt only oppression and the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] emanating from the ship.<ref name="DE" />

Leia met with Palpatine and her brother Luke Skywalker in a room much farther aft, close to the bridge. Leia, despite Palpatine's efforts, was able to convince Luke to turn against the Emperor. In response, Palpatine summoned a [[Force storm (wormhole)|Force storm]], an enormous conflagration of dark side energy, and turned it on the massed New Republic fleet.<ref name="DE" /> Using their own powers of the Force, Luke and Leia created a [[Force harmony|wave]] of [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] energy that stunned Palpatine and disrupted his control of the storm.<ref name="EGVV" /> The Force storm, its energies no longer directed by Palpatine, began consuming the ''Eclipse''. As the pair of Jedi escaped the doomed flagship, the storm destroyed the last of it.<ref name="DE" />

===Post-destruction===
After Palpatine's return from his second death, he utilized a replacement vessel of the same class as the original, christened the "''[[Eclipse II]]''," until it was destroyed by [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] sending it on a collision course with the [[Galaxy Gun]].<ref name="EE">''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' describes the ''Eclipse'' as "ten miles long from stem to stern"; the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' and subsequent sources have clarified its length as 17.5 kilometers, or slightly less than eleven miles.

The total design and construction time of the ''Eclipse'' is uncertain. Most sources, such as the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'', indicated that it had been under construction ever since the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. However, ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'', and ''[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]'', which references the game, show the ''Eclipse'' as already being under construction since the Battle of Yavin. Additionally, ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' states that the ship was in construction as long as either Death Star; since the publication of that and other sources that repeat the same information, the Death Star I was revealed to have been under construction for at least 19 years. However, the [[2007]] edition of [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'']] explicitly states that the ''Eclipse'' began construction around the time of the Battle of Yavin.
